_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning how literary devices, language features, dialogue and visual elements are used to create meaning and emotion in literary texts.

_source.w: We are learning how literary devices, language features, dialogue and visual elements are used to create meaning and emotion in literary texts.
y: Understanding these techniques helps us appreciate the craft of storytelling and interpret texts more deeply.
Recognising how emotion is constructed in texts makes us more discerning and engaged readers.
These analytical skills prepare us for more advanced literary study.
eg: We analyse how dialogue reveals character personality, relationships and conflict.
We examine how an author uses literary devices like foreshadowing to build suspense.
We discuss how still and moving images in a graphic novel or film adaptation create meaning alongside text.
We identify the language features that create emotional responses — like short sentences for shock or flowing descriptions for calm.
We compare how different authors achieve similar emotional effects through different techniques.
We apply our understanding of these techniques in our own creative writing.
